bead.title: readiness: tolerate scalar waiver criteria
bead.description:
PROBLEM
After `ddx-c9b6fb51` landed, the next live readiness check still produced a warn-only decode failure on 2026-05-15 while evaluating `ddx-5cc92777`: `decode readiness classification result: json: cannot unmarshal string into Go struct field preClaimReadinessClassificationPromptResult.waivers_applied.criteria of type []string`. The previous fix tolerated scalar `waivers_applied` and flat string arrays, but not a waiver object whose `criteria` field is a scalar string.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
`cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:223-229` defines `preClaimReadinessWaiver.Criteria` as `[]string`. `cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:272-279` unmarshals object entries directly into that strict struct before `normalizeReadinessStringList` can run, so legacy/model output like `{"waivers_applied":[{"reason":"docs-only","criteria":"docs-only","evidence":"..."}]}` fails the entire readiness check. Existing coverage at `cli/internal/agent/readiness_classification_test.go:291-304` only covers object waivers with `criteria` already encoded as an array.

PROPOSED FIX
Add a small custom unmarshal path for waiver object entries so `criteria` accepts either a JSON string or a JSON string array and normalizes to `[]string`. Keep `criteria` requested as an array in prompts and skill text; this is compatibility-only tolerance for legacy/model output. Do not loosen decisive readiness fields.

NON-SCOPE
Do not use waiver metadata for routing, power inference, ordering, or bead metadata. Do not add another readiness schema path. Do not change warn-only readiness policy, timeout behavior, or canonical readiness classifications.
bead.acceptance:
1. Add `TestPreClaimReadiness_DecodesWaiversAppliedObjectScalarCriteria` proving classification-shaped readiness JSON with `"waivers_applied":[{"reason":"docs-only","criteria":"docs-only","evidence":"fixture"}]` decodes without an unavailable-readiness error, normalizes criteria to `[]string{"docs-only"}`, and still applies the classification result.
2. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run 'TestPreClaimReadiness_DecodesWaiversAppliedObjectScalarCriteria' -count=1` passes.
3. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
